export.c(3) Library Functions Manual export.c(3)

NAME

export.c - GPG public key export implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int gpg_get_public_key (const char *key_id, uint8_t *public_key_out, char *keygrip_out)

Detailed Description

GPG public key export implementation.

Definition in file export.c.

Function Documentation

int gpg_get_public_key (const char * key_id, uint8_t * public_key_out, char * keygrip_out)

Definition at line 253 of file export.c.

253                                                                                        {
254   if (!key_id || !public_key_out) {
255     log_error("Invalid arguments to gpg_get_public_key");
256     return -1;
257   }
258 
259   // SECURITY: Validate key_id to prevent command injection
260   // GPG key IDs should be hexadecimal (0-9, a-f, A-F)
261   if (!validate_shell_safe(key_id, NULL)) {
262     log_error("Invalid GPG key ID format - contains unsafe characters: %s", key_id);
263     return -1;
264   }
265 
266   // Additional validation: ensure key_id is hex alphanumeric
267   for (size_t i = 0; key_id[i] != '\0'; i++) {
268     if (!isxdigit((unsigned char)key_id[i])) {
269       log_error("Invalid GPG key ID format - must be hexadecimal: %s", key_id);
270       return -1;
271     }
272   }
273 
274   // Escape key_id for safe use in shell command (single quotes)
275   char escaped_key_id[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
276   if (!escape_shell_single_quotes(key_id, escaped_key_id, sizeof(escaped_key_id))) {
277     log_error("Failed to escape GPG key ID for shell command");
278     return -1;
279   }
280 
281   // Use gpg to list the key and get the keygrip
282   char cmd[BUFFER_SIZE_LARGE];
283   safe_snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), "gpg --list-keys --with-keygrip --with-colons 0x%s " PLATFORM_SHELL_NULL_REDIRECT,
284                 escaped_key_id);
285 
286   FILE *fp = NULL;
287   if (platform_popen("gpg_export", cmd, "r", &fp) != ASCIICHAT_OK || !fp) {
288     log_error("Failed to run gpg command - GPG may not be installed");
289 #ifdef _WIN32
290     log_error("To install GPG on Windows, download Gpg4win from:");
291     log_error("  https://www.gpg4win.org/download.html");
292 #elif defined(__APPLE__)
293     log_error("To install GPG on macOS, use Homebrew:");
294     log_error("  brew install gnupg");
295 #else
296     log_error("To install GPG on Linux:");
297     log_error("  Debian/Ubuntu: sudo apt-get install gnupg");
298     log_error("  Fedora/RHEL:   sudo dnf install gnupg2");
299     log_error("  Arch Linux:    sudo pacman -S gnupg");
300     log_error("  Alpine Linux:  sudo apk add gnupg");
301 #endif
302     return -1;
303   }
304 
305   char line[BUFFER_SIZE_XLARGE];
306   char found_keygrip[128] = {0};
307   bool found_key = false;
308 
309   // Parse gpg output
310   // Format: pub:..., grp:::::::::<keygrip>:
311   while (fgets(line, sizeof(line), fp)) {
312     if (strncmp(line, "pub:", 4) == 0) {
313       // Found the public key line
314       found_key = true;
315     } else if (found_key && strncmp(line, "grp:", 4) == 0) {
316       // Extract keygrip using PCRE2 regex
317       // Format: grp:::::::::D52FF935FBA59609EE65E1685287828242A1EA1A:
318       char *keygrip_extracted = NULL;
319 
320       if (crypto_regex_extract_gpg_keygrip(line, &keygrip_extracted)) {
321         // Successfully extracted keygrip
322         SAFE_STRNCPY(found_keygrip, keygrip_extracted, sizeof(found_keygrip));
323         if (keygrip_out) {
324           SAFE_STRNCPY(keygrip_out, found_keygrip, 41);
325         }
326         SAFE_FREE(keygrip_extracted);
327       } else {
328         // Fallback to manual parsing if regex fails
329         const char *grp_start = line + 4;
330         int colon_count = 0;
331         while (*grp_start && colon_count < 8) {
332           if (*grp_start == ':') {
333             colon_count++;
334           }
335           grp_start++;
336         }
337 
338         if (colon_count == 8) {
339           const char *grp_end = strchr(grp_start, ':');
340           if (grp_end) {
341             size_t grp_len = grp_end - grp_start;
342             if (grp_len < sizeof(found_keygrip)) {
343               memcpy(found_keygrip, grp_start, grp_len);
344               found_keygrip[grp_len] = '\0';
345 
346               if (keygrip_out) {
347                 SAFE_STRNCPY(keygrip_out, found_keygrip, 41);
348               }
349             }
350           }
351         }
352       }
353       break;
354     }
355   }
356 
357   platform_pclose(&fp);
358 
359   if (!found_key || strlen(found_keygrip) == 0) {
360     log_error("Could not find GPG key with ID: %s", key_id);
361     return -1;
362   }
363 
364   log_debug("Found keygrip for key %s: %s", key_id, found_keygrip);
365 
366   // Try to use GPG agent API to read the public key directly via READKEY command
367   int agent_sock = gpg_agent_connect();
368   if (agent_sock < 0) {
369     log_debug("GPG agent not available, falling back to gpg --export for public key extraction");
370     // Fallback: Use gpg --export to get the public key
371     int export_result = gpg_export_public_key(key_id, public_key_out);
372     if (export_result == 0) {
373       log_debug("Successfully extracted public key using fallback method");
374     } else {
375       log_error("Fallback public key extraction failed for key ID: %s", key_id);
376     }
377     return export_result;
378   }
379 
380   // Send READKEY command with keygrip to get the public key S-expression
381   char readkey_cmd[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
382   safe_snprintf(readkey_cmd, sizeof(readkey_cmd), "READKEY %s\n", found_keygrip);
383 
384   // Cast agent_sock back to pipe_t (gpg_agent_connect returns pipe_t cast to int for portability)
385   pipe_t agent_pipe = (pipe_t)(intptr_t)agent_sock;
386   ssize_t bytes_written = platform_pipe_write(agent_pipe, (const unsigned char *)readkey_cmd, strlen(readkey_cmd));
387   if (bytes_written != (ssize_t)strlen(readkey_cmd)) {
388     log_error("Failed to send READKEY command to GPG agent");
389     gpg_agent_disconnect(agent_sock);
390     return -1;
391   }
392 
393   // Read the response (public key S-expression)
394   char response[BUFFER_SIZE_XXXLARGE];
395   memset(response, 0, sizeof(response));
396   ssize_t bytes_read = platform_pipe_read(agent_pipe, (unsigned char *)response, sizeof(response) - 1);
397 
398   gpg_agent_disconnect(agent_sock);
399 
400   if (bytes_read <= 0) {
401     log_error("Failed to read READKEY response from GPG agent");
402     return -1;
403   }
404 
405   // Parse the S-expression to extract Ed25519 public key (q value)
406   // GPG agent returns binary S-expressions in format: (1:q<length>:<binary-data>)
407   // Example: (1:q33:<33-bytes>) where first byte is 0x40 (Ed25519 prefix), then 32-byte key
408   const char *q_marker = strstr(response, "(1:q");
409   if (!q_marker) {
410     log_warn("Failed to find public key (1:q) in GPG agent READKEY response, trying gpg --export fallback");
411     log_debug("Response was: %.*s", (int)(bytes_read < 200 ? bytes_read : 200), response);
412     gpg_agent_disconnect(agent_sock);
413 
414     // Fallback: Use gpg --export for public-only keys
415     int export_result = gpg_export_public_key(key_id, public_key_out);
416     if (export_result == 0) {
417       log_debug("Successfully extracted public key using gpg --export fallback");
418     } else {
419       log_error("Fallback public key extraction failed for key ID: %s", key_id);
420     }
421     return export_result;
422   }
423 
424   // Skip "(1:q" to get to the length field
425   const char *len_start = q_marker + 4;
426 
427   // Parse the length (e.g., "33:")
428   char *colon = strchr(len_start, ':');
429   if (!colon) {
430     log_error("Malformed S-expression: missing colon after length");
431     return -1;
432   }
433 
434   size_t key_len = strtoul(len_start, NULL, 10);
435   if (key_len != 33) {
436     log_error("Unexpected Ed25519 public key length: %zu bytes (expected 33)", key_len);
437     return -1;
438   }
439 
440   // Skip the colon to get to the binary data
441   const unsigned char *binary_start = (const unsigned char *)(colon + 1);
442 
443   // Ed25519 public keys in GPG format have a 0x40 prefix byte, then 32 bytes of actual key
444   if (binary_start[0] != 0x40) {
445     log_error("Invalid Ed25519 public key prefix: 0x%02x (expected 0x40)", binary_start[0]);
446     return -1;
447   }
448 
449   // Copy the 32-byte public key (skip the 0x40 prefix)
450   memcpy(public_key_out, binary_start + 1, 32);
451 
452   log_debug("Extracted Ed25519 public key from GPG agent via READKEY command");
453   return 0;
454 }




References bytes_written,
    crypto_regex_extract_gpg_keygrip(),
    escape_shell_single_quotes(), gpg_agent_connect(),
    gpg_agent_disconnect(), safe_snprintf(), and
    validate_shell_safe().


Referenced by extract_ed25519_from_gpg(), and
    parse_private_key().
